import statistics
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

def task_func(sales_data):
    N = len(sales_data)  # number of products
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()  # create a figure with an axes object

    # calculate the mean and standard deviation for each product's sales data
    means = [statistics.mean(product['sales']) for product in sales_data]
    stdevs = [statistics.stdev(product['sales']) for product in sales_data]

    # create a new figure with the same axes for easy plotting of multiple lines
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()

    # iterate over each product's data and plot it on the axes
    for i, product in enumerate(sales_data):
        ax.plot(product['month'], product['sales'], label=f"Product {i+1}")
        # shading the area within mean +/- standard deviation
        lower = [mean - stdev for mean, stdev in zip(means, stdevs)]
        upper = [mean + stdev for mean, stdev in zip(means, stdevs)]
        ax.fill_between(product['month'], lower, upper, alpha=0.2)

    # set the title and labels
    ax.set_title("Sales trends over a year")
    ax.set_xlabel('Month')
    ax.set_ylabel('Sales')
    ax.legend()

    return fig, ax
